title: Latin Cognates - ION Endings
description: Learn how to convert English words ending in -ion to Spanish, and explore basic verb forms.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: How would you say 'urgent'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Urgente. Good. How would you say 'urgently'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Urgentemente. Good. 'I love you' in Spanish is 'te quiero', literally 'I want you'. 'Te amo' is more serious. 'Te' is 'you', so 'quiero' means 'I want'. How would you say 'I want'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero. Good. How would you say 'I don't want'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No quiero. Good. Words ending in -ion in English are similar in Spanish, just stress the last syllable. How would you say 'opinion'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Opinión. Good. How about 'passion'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Pasión. Good. Remember to pronounce the S clearly. How would you say 'organization'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Organización. Good. How about 'preparation'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Preparación. Good. Now 'generation'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Generación. Good. How would you say 'confirmation'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Confirmación. Good. Now let's look at verbs. If you remove -ción and add -r, you get the verb. How would you say 'to generate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Generar. Good. How about 'to confirm'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Confirmar. Good. How would you say 'to sign'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Firmar. Good. Now 'complication'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Complicación. And 'to complicate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Complicar. Good. Let's make some sentences. How would you say 'I want to prepare'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero preparar. Good. How do you say 'cooperation'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Cooperación. And 'to cooperate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Cooperar. Good. How would you say 'I want to cooperate'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero cooperar. Good. How do you say 'cancellation'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: And 'to cancel'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Cancelar. Good. How would you say 'I want to cancel'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero cancelar. Good. How would you say 'I don't want to cancel'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: No quiero cancelar. Perfect. How do you say 'exploration'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: And 'to explore'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Explorar. Good. How would you say 'I want to explore'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero explorar. Perfect. The word for 'me' in Spanish is 'me'. How would you say 'I want to prepare myself'?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quiero prepararme. Good. In English, Latin words can sound formal, but in Spanish, they sound normal.

outro.text: You've learned how to convert English words ending in -ion to Spanish by stressing the last syllable.
Try converting some other -ion words like 'celebration' or 'imagination' to Spanish.
You can now form basic sentences using these words and verbs. Practice saying what you want to do or don't want to do.
Next time, we'll explore more patterns to expand your vocabulary even further.
